purpose: Generate the final Young G+P prediction files from input data using frozen student parameters selected during the original optimization campaign.

auxiliary_distillation_signal.description: Prior Young G+P prediction sets from the modeling campaign were aligned by subject ID and distilled into a consensus teacher signal. PHQ-9 values are averaged and binary/ternary labels are selected by weighted voting.
auxiliary_distillation_signal.source_type: team-owned archived prediction artifacts from train-only model and ensemble variants

selection_rule: minimize class mismatch count against the distilled prediction-bank teacher audit
require PHQ predictions to stay within tolerance
prefer the largest ridge regularization alpha among acceptable candidates
use PHQ mean absolute error only as the final tie-breaker
optimization_campaign.iteration_budget_note: The selected checkpoint is the most regularized acceptable candidate from a 100-point deterministic grid. Prior prediction artifacts are only needed to rebuild the teacher distillation signal; they are not required for inference.

generalization_note: The original modeling campaign used train-only cross-validation, model ensembling, teacher-bank distillation, and stability-oriented search. This delivery package freezes the final selected student parameterization/checkpoint so the prediction files can be generated without the original large checkpoints.
